meta.title: Greene Family Heritage
meta.subtitle: Tracing the Greene lineage from Portarlington to Dublin and beyond

people.name: Thos Green
people.notes: Earliest known ancestor. Appears in Tithe Applotment Books for Ballyburly parish, King's County, 1830s. Approximately 8km north of Portarlington.
sources: Tithe Applotment Books, Ballyburly parish, King's County, 1830-36

people.name: Joseph Greene
people.notes: Confirmed father of Mary Bridget Greene (b.1874, Portarlington). Married Elizabeth Foran. May be connected to Lucy Greene (widow in Kilmalogue, 1901 census). NOTE (April 2026): an earlier hypothesis that Joseph was also Thomas Christopher Greene's father has been SUPERSEDED — the 1922 civil marriage register for Thomas × Bridget Clarke names Thomas's father as WILLIAM Greene, not Joseph. Joseph and William may yet prove to be related (e.g. brothers, both from Portarlington), but on present evidence Joseph is the confirmed father of Mary Bridget only.
sources: Ancestry record for Mary Bridget Greene (b.1874, d.1966, Queens NY)
IrishGenealogy.ie civil marriage register cima-1271568 (1922) — names Thomas Greene's father as WILLIAM Greene, NOT Joseph; supersedes the earlier inference of Thomas as Joseph's son

people.name: Elizabeth Foran
people.notes: Wife of Joseph Greene. Mother of Mary Bridget Greene (b.1874, Portarlington). Maiden name Foran.
sources: Ancestry record for Mary Bridget Greene

people.name: Lucy Greene
people.notes: Widow by 1901, living in Kilmalogue, Portarlington North, King's Co. Possibly Joseph Greene's widow or a relative. Had daughter Lizzie who married Christopher Gannon. Absent from 1911 census (likely deceased).
sources: 1901 Census: Kilmalogue, Portarlington North, King's Co., House 16

people.name: William Greene
people.notes: Father of Thomas Christopher Greene. CONFIRMED (April 2026) from the 1922 civil marriage register (Dundrum, Rathdown RD, record cima-1271568) which names Thomas's father as "William Greene, Traveller". "Traveller" in this context almost certainly means commercial traveller (travelling salesman) rather than a member of the Irish Traveller community — a respectable late-Victorian / Edwardian occupation that involved going from town to town selling goods on commission. CANDIDATE IDENTITIES (not yet narrowed): two William Greenes appear in our prior Portarlington research and either could be him: (1) WILLIAM GREENE OF IMACRANNAGH — a farmer in the immediate vicinity of Portarlington, recorded with "his large family"; this is a strong geographical and generational fit for Thomas's father (Thomas b.c.1870 Portarlington), and a man could plausibly be a farmer in mid-life and a commercial traveller in later years; (2) WILLIAM GREENE OF SHANDERRY — listed as a labourer with the census note "husband working in Dublin", which is intriguing given Thomas's adult life as a Dublin warehouseman/porter. Civil records (a marriage record for William Greene to a yet-unknown wife, baptismal records of Thomas and any siblings in Portarlington Catholic parish registers, William's death certificate) are needed to settle the identity. NOTE: the earlier hypothesis that Joseph Greene (confirmed father of Mary Bridget Greene b.1874 Portarlington) was also Thomas's father is SUPERSEDED by this 1922 marriage record evidence. Joseph and William may yet prove related (siblings, cousins) — both being in Portarlington at the same time — but they are evidently distinct men.
sources: IrishGenealogy.ie civil marriage register, Dundrum (Rathdown RD), 20 June 1922 — Thomas Greene (Widower, Warehouseman, of [Dublin]) × Bridget Clarke (Spinster, Housekeeper, of Drummartin House, Dundrum). Father of Thomas: WILLIAM GREENE (Traveller). Father of Bridget: PATRICK CLARKE (Farmer). Witnesses: Patrick Kennedy & Annie O'Malley. Officiant: Richard Duggan, P.P. Holy Cross, Dundrum. Record id cima-1271568. https://www.irishgenealogy.ie/view?record_id=cima-1271568
Prior research: William Greene of Imacrannagh, Portarlington area (farmer, with a large family) — see portarlington.html
Prior research: William Greene of Shanderry, Co. Laois (labourer; census note "husband working in Dublin") — see portarlington.html

people.name: Thomas Christopher Greene
people.occupation: Shop Porter (1901), Gentleman's Club Steward (1911), Shop Porter — Switzers & Co. Drapers (1926), Warehouse Porter (1951)
people.notes: Central figure of this research. Full name Thomas Christopher Greene (discovered from Ancestry marriage index). Born c. July 1870 in Portarlington (age 55y 9m in the April 1926 Census; age 81 at death in December 1951). Moved to Dublin by 1899. Lied about his age to enlist in the British Army at around age 14 (~1884) — likely the Prince of Wales's Leinster Regiment (Royal Canadians), headquartered at Birr Barracks, ~25 miles from Portarlington. Underage enlistment was common in the late Victorian army. May have served during the Boer War (1899-1902). Married twice: first to Elizabeth (d. c.1912-1925), then to Bridget Clarke. Marriage registered Dublin South, 1899, Vol 2, Page 593. In 1926, living at York Street, Dublin, working as Shop Porter at Switzers & Co. Drapers — the famous Grafton Street department store founded in 1838, one of Dublin's most prestigious retail institutions (later became Brown Thomas in 1995). Thomas would have walked the 10 minutes from York Street to Grafton Street each morning. His stepson Patrick (age 23, from first marriage) was also living in the household. Family moved to 38 Goldenbridge Avenue, Inchicore in the early-to-mid 1930s — part of Dublin Corporation's ambitious social housing programme replacing inner-city tenements with purpose-built suburban homes. Died 6 December 1951 at 38 Goldenbridge Avenue, age 81. Cause of death: carcinoma of stomach, cachexia, cardiac failure. Death registered by his son John Greene. Still listed as 'Warehouse Porter' at death — a working man to the end. FATHER CONFIRMED (April 2026, 1922 marriage register): Thomas's father is WILLIAM GREENE, occupation "Traveller" (almost certainly commercial traveller / travelling salesman). The 1922 marriage record names this directly. This corrects an earlier speculative parentage of Joseph Greene & Elizabeth Foran (which was based only on Joseph being the confirmed father of Mary Bridget Greene b.1874 in the same town); Joseph and William may yet prove related (siblings or cousins), but the direct documentary evidence names William. There are TWO William Greenes in our prior Portarlington research who could be this man: William Greene of Imacrannagh (farmer, with a "large family" — geographically and generationally a strong match for Thomas's father) and William Greene of Shanderry (labourer; census note "husband working in Dublin" — also intriguing given Thomas's adult life in Dublin). Identity not yet narrowed between the two.
sources: 1901 Census: Ranelagh Avenue, Rathmines & Rathgar East, Dublin (ID 1293881)
1911 Census: Clonliffe Avenue, Mountjoy, Dublin (ID 15408)
1926 Census: York Street, Dublin — Thomas Greene, Head, age 55y 9m, Shop Porter, Switzers & Co. Drapers
Death Certificate: Entry 486, 6 December 1951, registered Dublin South, Registrar M. Ryan

IrishGenealogy.ie civil marriage register, Dundrum (Rathdown RD), 20 June 1922 — Thomas Greene (Widower, Warehouseman, of [Dublin]) × Bridget Clarke (Spinster, Housekeeper, of Drummartin House, Dundrum). Father of Thomas: WILLIAM GREENE (Traveller). Father of Bridget: PATRICK CLARKE (Farmer). Witnesses: Patrick Kennedy & Annie O'Malley. Officiant: Richard Duggan, P.P. Holy Cross, Dundrum. Record id cima-1271568. https://www.irishgenealogy.ie/view?record_id=cima-1271568

people.name: Elizabeth Greene (née unknown)
people.notes: First wife of Thomas Greene. Approximately the same age as Thomas (he 40, she 41 in the April 1911 Census — born c.1870 in Co. Dublin). Married c.1899 (12 years married in 1911 census). Three children born, three living in 1911: Thomas Jr (10), Patrick (8), Elizabeth (4). Maiden name unknown — her marriage record on IrishGenealogy.ie would reveal it. Her death (c.1912-1925) cleared the way for Thomas's second marriage to Bridget Clarke.
sources: 1901 Census: Ranelagh Avenue, Rathmines, Dublin

1911 Census Form A (Form B No. 1842): Clonliffe Avenue, Mountjoy, Dublin — Elizabeth Greene, Wife, age 41, RC, born Co. Dublin, 12 years married, 3 born / 3 living. Signed by head Thomas Greene; enumerator John Broderick.

people.name: Bridget Greene (née Clarke)
people.occupation: Cook / Head Servant (in service before marriage), Housewife
people.notes: Second wife of Thomas Greene. Maiden name Clarke. Born 2 December 1891 in Mullaghfin, Duleek, County Meath — CONFIRMED (April 2026) from her civil birth register (Duleek RD entry 107, registered 26 Dec 1891). Father: Patrick Clarke, labourer of Mullaghfin. Mother: Catherine née CLINTON. The four prior triangulating records — 1901 Census (age 9), 1911 Census (age 19, Cook for the Dollard JP household), 1926 Census (age 34y 4m, given to the month), and her death certificate (age 78 in 1970) — all align perfectly with the 2 December 1891 birth. 1901 CENSUS: Bridget Clarke (age 9, Scholar, Roman Catholic) recorded in House 3, Mullaghfin townland, Duleek DED, County Meath — daughter of Catherine Clarke (age 40, widow, General Labourer). Siblings: Rose (23), Patrick (18), Mary (12), Jane (10), Agnes (5). Her father Patrick Clarke had died sometime between Bridget's birth (Dec 1891, when he was alive as the labourer recorded on her birth register) and the 1901 Census (when Catherine was already a widow). 1911 CENSUS — CONFIRMED (April 2026): Bridget Clarke (age 19, Single, Roman Catholic, Read & Write, born Co. Meath) is working as COOK (Domestic) in the Dublin household of Joseph Dollard, Justice of the Peace and Printer-Master (Form A, Form B No. 142, enumerated 2 April 1911 by Robert J. Holmes). 1922 MARRIAGE — CONFIRMED (April 2026): Bridget Clarke married Thomas Greene on 20 June 1922 at the Roman Catholic Church of Holy Cross, Dundrum, in the Registrar's District of Dundrum (Union of Rathdown, Co. Dublin). Bridget was a Spinster, Housekeeper, of Drummartin House, Dundrum. Thomas was a Widower, Warehouseman, of an address in Dublin city. Her father Patrick Clarke is recorded on the register as "Farmer" (occupation slightly different from the 1891 birth register's "Labourer" — possibly reflecting later land-holding, or simply how Bridget remembered/described her late father). Witnesses: Patrick Kennedy and Annie O'Malley. Officiant: Richard Duggan, P.P. Officiating Registrar: J. Sutton, Deputy Registrar of Marriages, Dundrum. The Holy Cross church and Drummartin House sit a short walk from each other in Dundrum — Bridget had clearly settled into domestic service in this affluent south Dublin suburb before her marriage. By 1926 she had moved with Thomas to York Street, Dublin (1926 Census: Wife, age 34y 4m, Home Duties, born Meath/Duleek). Mother of John, Thomas, Brendan, and Ita (Mark's grandmother-in-law). Family moved to 38 Goldenbridge Avenue, Inchicore in the early 1930s. Died 9 August 1970, age 78, Adelaide Hospital, Dublin. Death registered by son Brendan Greene, 62 Limekiln Drive, Terenure.
sources: IrishGenealogy.ie civil birth register, Duleek (Drogheda RD, Co. Meath), entry 107 — Bridget Clarke born 2 December 1891 at Mullaghfin. Father: Pat Clark, Mullaghfin, Labourer. Mother: Catherine Clarke formerly CLINTON. Informant: Julia Boylan (her mark), present at birth. Registered 26 December 1891 by James Kelly, Registrar. Record id civbi-9688899. https://www.irishgenealogy.ie/view?record_id=civbi-9688899

IrishGenealogy.ie civil marriage register, Dundrum (Rathdown RD), 20 June 1922 — Thomas Greene (Widower, Warehouseman, of [Dublin]) × Bridget Clarke (Spinster, Housekeeper, of Drummartin House, Dundrum). Father of Thomas: WILLIAM GREENE (Traveller). Father of Bridget: PATRICK CLARKE (Farmer). Witnesses: Patrick Kennedy & Annie O'Malley. Officiant: Richard Duggan, P.P. Holy Cross, Dundrum. Record id cima-1271568. https://www.irishgenealogy.ie/view?record_id=cima-1271568

1901 Census of Ireland: House 3 Mullaghfin, Duleek DED, Co. Meath — Bridget Clarke, age 9, Scholar, daughter of Catherine Clarke (widow). Roman Catholic. Born Co. Meath. http://www.census.nationalarchives.ie/pages/1901/Meath/Duleek/Mullaghfin/1609301/

1911 Census of Ireland (CONFIRMED April 2026, Form A image — Form B No. 142, enumerated 2 April 1911 by Robert J. Holmes): Joseph Dollard household, Dublin — Bridget Clarke, Servant, age 19, Cook (Domestic), Single, Roman Catholic, Read & Write, born Co. Meath. Co-resident: Joseph Dollard (Head, 40, JP & Printer-Master, Co. Dublin), Sarah Dollard (Wife, 34, King's Co.), Mary (14), Kathleen (13), Sarah (3 mo.), Nursery Governess Ellen Clancy (24, Co. Meath), Housemaid Margaret Brien (20, Co. Wexford)
1926 Census of the Irish Free State: York Street, Dublin — Bridget Greene, Wife, age 34 years 4 months, Home Duties, born Meath/Duleek

Death Certificate: Entry 22, 9 August 1970, Adelaide Hospital, Dublin — Bridget Greene, age 78, Widow, late of 38 Goldenbridge Avenue, Inchicore. Registered by son Brendan Greene, 62 Limekiln Drive, Terenure

Family oral history (Garrigan/Donnelly family, transmitted via Ita Greene/Donnelly): Bridget worked as a cook / head-of-household servant in service in Dublin before her marriage to Thomas Greene — corroborated by both the 1911 Census Form A (Cook in Joseph Dollard's household) and the 1922 marriage register (Housekeeper at Drummartin House, Dundrum)

people.name: Thomas Greene Jr
people.notes: Eldest child of Thomas and Elizabeth (first wife).
sources: 1911 Census: Clonliffe Avenue, Mountjoy, Dublin — age 10

people.name: Patrick Greene
people.occupation: Scholar (1911), Battery Repairer / Motor Engineer — S.F. Robinson (1926)
people.notes: Second child of Thomas and Elizabeth (first wife). In the 1926 Census, Patrick (age 23, single) is still living with his father's second family at York Street, Dublin. He works as a Battery Repairer / Motor Engineer for S.F. Robinson — a skilled trade in the early motor age. This is significant: it shows the first-marriage children maintained a relationship with Thomas after his remarriage.
sources: 1911 Census: Clonliffe Avenue, Mountjoy, Dublin — age 8
1926 Census: York Street, Dublin — Patrick Greene, Son, age 23, Single, Battery Repairer/Motor Engineer, S.F. Robinson

people.name: Elizabeth Bessie Harris (née Greene)
people.notes: Daughter of Thomas and Elizabeth (first wife). Full name Elizabeth Bessie Greene. MAJOR DISCOVERY (Apr 2026): A primary-source marriage certificate from the Parish of St. Helier, Jersey — Page 97, 1941 — confirms she married Alfred Bower Harris on 13 September 1941, AT THE HEIGHT OF THE GERMAN OCCUPATION of the Channel Islands (occupation began 30 June 1940, so they wed 14 months in). She was 34, a Spinster, working as a Nurse, residing at St. Saviour's, Jersey. Alfred was 28, a Bachelor, Printer, also of St. Saviour's, born Bretforton, Evesham, Worcestershire. The marriage was solemnized at the Church of St Mary and St Peter, Vauxhall (a Catholic church in Jersey), by certificate of the Superintendent Registrar — officiant Richard T. Arnett. Witnesses were F.C. Colligny and C.M. Colligny; Deputy Registrar W.A. de la Perrelle. Her father Thomas Greene is recorded as 'Retired' (confirming he was alive in 1941). This places her on JERSEY, not Guernsey as previously assumed — all Channel Islands research should pivot. Working as a nurse during the German Occupation (1940-1945). Deported c.1942 and interned at Biberach Internment Camp (Ilag V-B), Germany. Worked for two years at the Men's Hospital under Dr. A.W.H. Donaldson. Letter of recommendation written 21 May 1945, four weeks after liberation by French forces.
sources: 1911 Census: Clonliffe Avenue, Mountjoy, Dublin — age 4

MARRIAGE CERTIFICATE (PRIMARY SOURCE): Extract from the Register of Marriages, Parish of St. Helier, Island of Jersey, Page 97, 1941. Marriage of Alfred Bower Harris and Elizabeth Bessie Greene, 13 September 1941. Held in family papers.
Letter of recommendation from Dr. A.W.H. Donaldson, M.B., D.P.H., Biberach Internment Camp, 21 May 1945

Arolsen Archives online search attempted (collections.arolsen-archives.org) on 2026 for variants 'Elizabeth Harris', 'Elizabeth Greene', 'Bessie Harris', 'Alfred Harris' (with/without Biberach filter, document-type 2 = persons). The site is a JS single-page app whose backing service (collections-server.arolsen-archives.org/ITS-WS.asmx) only responds to authenticated SPA calls; results could not be retrieved by direct URL or API probing. NO match confirmed and NO match disproved — search remains OPEN. Recommended next step: submit a free inquiry form to Arolsen Archives staff (https://arolsen-archives.org/en/search-explore/search-online-archive/) with full details (Elizabeth Bessie Harris née Greene, b. Dublin c.1907, m. Alfred Bower Harris 13 Sept 1941 Jersey, interned Biberach Ilag V-B), and cross-check 'Islanders Deported' by Roger E. Harris which contains the complete Biberach internee list.

people.name: Alfred Bower Harris
people.notes: Husband of Elizabeth Bessie Greene. Born c.1913 in Bretforton, a Worcestershire village near Evesham. Working as a Printer in St. Saviour's, Jersey by 1941 — how an English printer ended up on Jersey before the war is unknown (perhaps work, perhaps already there when the German Army arrived 30 June 1940). Aged 28 and a Bachelor when he married Elizabeth Greene on 13 September 1941, during the German Occupation. His father Charles Edward Harris was already deceased by the marriage. As an English-born civilian, Alfred would have been a primary target of the September 1942 deportation orders that sent ~2,300 Channel Islanders to internment camps.
sources: MARRIAGE CERTIFICATE (PRIMARY SOURCE): Extract from the Register of Marriages, Parish of St. Helier, Island of Jersey, Page 97, 1941. Marriage of Alfred Bower Harris and Elizabeth Bessie Greene, 13 September 1941.

people.name: Charles Edward Harris
people.notes: Father of Alfred Bower Harris (Elizabeth Greene's husband). Recorded as 'Deceased' on his son's marriage certificate, 13 September 1941 — so died before that date. Family from Bretforton, Worcestershire, England.
sources: Marriage certificate of Alfred Bower Harris & Elizabeth Bessie Greene, Jersey, 13 September 1941 — father's name and 'deceased' status recorded

people.name: Mary Bridget Greene
people.notes: Daughter of Joseph Greene and Elizabeth Foran. Emigrated to the United States. If Thomas Greene is also a child of Joseph and Elizabeth Foran, Mary Bridget is Thomas's sister.
sources: Ancestry.com record: Mary Bridget Greene, b.1874, d.1966, Queens NY

people.name: William Greene
people.notes: Living in Shanderry, Portarlington North. In 1901, his wife Johanna noted 'Husband Working in Dublin' — paralleling Thomas's presence in Dublin. Married Johanna c.1888. Six children. Possible brother of Thomas Greene.
sources: 1901 Census: Shanderry, Portarlington North, King's Co. (wife Johanna as head)

people.name: John Greene
people.notes: Son of Thomas Greene and Bridget Clarke (second wife). In the 1926 Census at York Street, Dublin, age 2 years 9 months. Living at 38 Goldenbridge Avenue, Inchicore, Dublin by 1951. Registered his father Thomas's death on 7 December 1951.
sources: 1926 Census: York Street, Dublin — John Greene, Son, age 2y 9m
Death Certificate of Thomas Greene, Entry 486, 6 December 1951 — informant: John Greene, Son

people.name: Thomas Greene (II)
people.notes: Son of Thomas Greene and Bridget Clarke (second wife). Full name Thomas Patrick Greene — middle name Patrick likely after his half-brother who shared the household. In the 1926 Census at York Street, Dublin, age just 9 months. CONFIRMED by NZ BDM: Death registration 2000/1764 — Thomas Patrick Greene, Date of Birth 21 July 1925. Emigrated to New Zealand where he married Margaret, a Samoan woman. Their marriage is post-1951 (outside NZ BDM 75-year historical records window). Died in 2000 in New Zealand. Had a number of children, descendants now living in New Zealand and Australia.
sources: 1926 Census: York Street, Dublin — Thomas Greene, Son, age 0y 9m
NZ BDM Death Registration: 2000/1764 — Thomas Patrick Greene, DOB 21 July 1925
Family oral history

people.name: Brendan Greene
people.notes: Son of Thomas Greene and Bridget Clarke (second wife). Born after the 1926 Census. Living at 62 Limekiln Drive, Terenure, Dublin by 1970. Registered his mother Bridget's death on 11 August 1970. Married Roisin (predeceased — died before September 2022). Three children: Terence, Geraldine, and Dermot (predeceased — died 18 September 2022 at Our Lady's Hospice, Harold's Cross; late of Ratoath, Co. Meath and Terenure; had sons Kris and Daniel, partner Monica; funeral St Agnes' Church Crumlin, cremated Mount Jerome). Died 3 March 2023 at Limekiln Road, Terenure. Survived by his sister Ita.
sources: Family oral history
Death Certificate of Bridget Greene, Entry 22, 9 August 1970 — informant: Brendan Greene, Son, 62 Limekiln Drive, Terenure
RIP.ie death notice, March 2023

people.name: Ita Greene (Eithne)
people.notes: Youngest daughter of Thomas Greene and Bridget Clarke (second wife). Mark Garrigan's grandmother-in-law. Name 'Eita' / 'Ita' from the Irish name Eithne. Born 1934 in Dublin. Married John Donnelly (predeceased). Four children: Ann (m. Paul), John Jr (m. Josette), Sheila (m. Robbie), Ger (m. Emily). Nine grandchildren: Claire, Eoin, Laura, Emma, JJ, Christopher, Nicole, Lauren, Jack. Also had great-grandchildren. Remained at Goldenbridge Avenue, Inchicore — the family home — her entire life. Passed away peacefully on 6 December 2025 in Kiltipper Woods Care Centre, surrounded by her family. Repose at daughter Sheila's home. Funeral Mass at St. Michael's Church, Inchicore; buried Deansgrange Cemetery. Family requested donations to the Alzheimer Society of Ireland in her memory.
sources: Family oral history
RIP.ie death notice, December 2025

people.name: Claire Garrigan (née Donnelly)
people.notes: Daughter of Ann Donnelly. Wife of Mark Garrigan (the researcher behind this project). Great-granddaughter of Thomas Christopher Greene and Bridget Clarke. The direct family connection that inspired this heritage research.
sources: Family oral history

people.name: Mark Garrigan
people.notes: Husband of Claire Garrigan (née Donnelly). An utter legend. Researcher and creator of this heritage project. Connected to the Greene family through his wife Claire — great-granddaughter of Thomas Christopher Greene and Bridget Clarke.
sources: Family oral history

people.name: Catherine Clarke (née Clinton)
people.notes: Mother of Bridget Greene (née Clarke). MAIDEN NAME CLINTON — CONFIRMED (April 2026) from her daughter Bridget's 1891 civil birth register (Duleek RD entry 107), which records the mother as "Catherine Clarke formerly CLINTON". Husband: PATRICK CLARKE, labourer of Mullaghfin (also confirmed from the 1891 birth register; recorded as "Farmer" on the 1922 marriage register of his daughter Bridget × Thomas Greene). Found in BOTH the 1901 and 1911 Census at Mullaghfin, Duleek DED, County Meath. In 1901: age 40, widowed, General Labourer, could not read. Children: Rose (23), Patrick (18), Mary (12), Jane (10), Bridget (9 — born 2 December 1891 per civil birth register), Agnes (5). In 1911: age 55, still widowed, still at Mullaghfin (same House 3), now a Charwoman, still cannot read. By 1911 all her children had left home — Bridget (then 19) was working as Cook in the Dublin household of Joseph Dollard, JP & Printer-Master. Her husband Patrick Clarke had died sometime between December 1891 (when he was alive on Bridget's birth register) and 1901. RESEARCH LEAD (April 2026, unverified): A gravestone in Duleek Old Churchyard, transcribed by historian Enda O'Boyle and published in Riocht na Midhe 2001/2002, reads: "Erected by the children of Thomas and Bridget Clarke of Duleek in loving memory of their father who died 23 January 1877 aged 45 years and their mother who died 25th January 1882 aged 44 years. Also their brothers, Patrick who died 4 July 1897 aged 28 years and Richard who died 25 July 1937 aged 72 years." The dates rule out the gravestone Patrick (b.~1869) being Bridget's father (he'd have been ~9 when Catherine's eldest Rose was born in 1878). But it remains plausible that Catherine's husband Patrick Clarke was a brother of these — i.e. that Thomas Clarke (d.1877) and his wife Bridget Clarke née ? of Duleek were Bridget Greene's paternal grandparents. The naming pattern (Catherine's daughter Bridget b.1891 named after the grandmother Bridget; her son Patrick b.~1883 named for the family line) supports this. Also a separate Phase A3 false lead worth recording: a Patrick Clarke × Catherine Kavanagh née McKenna marriage 25 Jan 1875 Navan (record cima-2861090) was the top hit of an early search but is NOT our family — those parties were a different age cohort (b.c.1833-35) and Bridget's 1891 birth register definitively names her mother as Clinton, not McKenna.
sources: IrishGenealogy.ie civil birth register, Duleek (Drogheda RD, Co. Meath), entry 107 — Bridget Clarke born 2 December 1891 at Mullaghfin. Father: Pat Clark, Mullaghfin, Labourer. Mother: Catherine Clarke formerly CLINTON. Informant: Julia Boylan (her mark), present at birth. Registered 26 December 1891 by James Kelly, Registrar. Record id civbi-9688899. https://www.irishgenealogy.ie/view?record_id=civbi-9688899

IrishGenealogy.ie civil marriage register, Dundrum (Rathdown RD), 20 June 1922 — Thomas Greene (Widower, Warehouseman, of [Dublin]) × Bridget Clarke (Spinster, Housekeeper, of Drummartin House, Dundrum). Father of Thomas: WILLIAM GREENE (Traveller). Father of Bridget: PATRICK CLARKE (Farmer). Witnesses: Patrick Kennedy & Annie O'Malley. Officiant: Richard Duggan, P.P. Holy Cross, Dundrum. Record id cima-1271568. https://www.irishgenealogy.ie/view?record_id=cima-1271568

NEGATIVE FINDING — IrishGenealogy.ie record cima-2861090 (Patrick Clarke × Catherine Kavanagh née McKenna, 25 January 1875, Navan): a top hit of the initial A3 search but NOT our family. The 1891 birth register for Bridget unambiguously gives her mother's maiden name as CLINTON (not McKenna). The 1875 Navan couple were also of a different age cohort (Patrick 42, Catherine 40 in 1875 → both born c.1833-35) and lived in Navan town corporation, not Mullaghfin/Duleek.
1901 Census: Mullaghfin, Duleek DED, County Meath — Catherine Clarke, Head, age 40, Widow, General Labourer, Roman Catholic
1911 Census: Mullaghfin, Duleek DED, County Meath — Catherine Clarke, Head, age 55, Widow, Charwoman, Roman Catholic, House 3

Research lead (unverified): Duleek Old Churchyard gravestone — Thomas Clarke (d.23 Jan 1877, age 45) & wife Bridget (d.25 Jan 1882, age 44), with sons Patrick (d.1897 age 28) and Richard (d.1937 age 72) — possible PATERNAL grandparents of Bridget Greene née Clarke. Source: https://meathhistoryhub.ie/duleek-old-churchyard/

people.name: Margaret Greene
people.notes: Wife of Thomas Patrick Greene (II). Samoan woman who married Thomas in New Zealand (post-1951, outside NZ BDM historical records window). Mother of Thomas and Margaret's children, descendants now in New Zealand and Australia.
sources: Family oral history

people.name: Rose Clarke
people.notes: Child of Catherine Clarke (widow) of Mullaghfin, Duleek DED, Co. Meath. Sibling of Bridget Clarke (later Bridget Greene, m. Thomas Christopher Greene). Recorded in 1901 Census of Ireland, House 3 Mullaghfin, age 23, General Labourer, literacy: Cannot read. Roman Catholic. Born Co. Meath. Further life unknown.
sources: 1901 Census of Ireland (PRIMARY SOURCE, retrieved 2026): Mullaghfin, Duleek DED, Co. Meath, House 3 — http://www.census.nationalarchives.ie/pages/1901/Meath/Duleek/Mullaghfin/1609301/ ; original Form A image: http://www.census.nationalarchives.ie/reels/nai001112178/

people.name: Patrick Clarke Snr

died.date: sometime between December 1891 and the 1901 Census

people.occupation: Labourer (per 1891 birth register of his daughter Bridget); recorded as "Farmer" on the 1922 marriage register of his daughter Bridget × Thomas Greene
people.notes: Husband of Catherine Clarke (née Clinton) of Mullaghfin, Duleek, Co. Meath. Father of at least six children: Rose (b.c.1878), Patrick (b.c.1883), Mary (b.c.1889), Jane (b.c.1891), Bridget (b. 2 December 1891), and Agnes (b.c.1896). His existence and identity are CONFIRMED (April 2026) from two civil records: (1) his daughter Bridget's 1891 civil birth register (Duleek RD entry 107) names him as "Pat Clark, Mullaghfin, Labourer"; (2) his daughter Bridget's 1922 civil marriage register (Dundrum, Rathdown RD, record cima-1271568) names him as "Patrick Clarke, Farmer" — i.e. Bridget's father is independently named on both records as Patrick Clarke. He was alive at Bridget's birth in December 1891 but had died by the 1901 Census (when Catherine is recorded as a widow). His marriage to Catherine Clinton is presumed to have taken place in Co. Meath in the 1870s (Rose, the eldest known child, was born c.1878). Under investigation: corrected A3 search dispatched April 2026 to find their marriage record. RESEARCH LEAD (unverified): the Duleek Old Churchyard gravestone (transcribed by Enda O'Boyle in Riocht na Midhe 2001/2002) names a Thomas Clarke (d.1877, age 45) and his wife Bridget Clarke (d.1882, age 44) of Duleek with children including Patrick (d.1897 age 28) and Richard (d.1937 age 72) — generationally these would fit being Patrick Clarke Snr's parents (i.e. Bridget Greene's paternal grandparents), but his birth/baptismal record is needed to confirm. The naming pattern is suggestive: the granddaughter Bridget (b.1891) was named for the grandmother Bridget Clarke.
sources: IrishGenealogy.ie civil birth register, Duleek (Drogheda RD, Co. Meath), entry 107 — Bridget Clarke born 2 December 1891 at Mullaghfin. Father: Pat Clark, Mullaghfin, Labourer. Mother: Catherine Clarke formerly CLINTON. Informant: Julia Boylan (her mark), present at birth. Registered 26 December 1891 by James Kelly, Registrar. Record id civbi-9688899. https://www.irishgenealogy.ie/view?record_id=civbi-9688899

IrishGenealogy.ie civil marriage register, Dundrum (Rathdown RD), 20 June 1922 — Thomas Greene (Widower, Warehouseman, of [Dublin]) × Bridget Clarke (Spinster, Housekeeper, of Drummartin House, Dundrum). Father of Thomas: WILLIAM GREENE (Traveller). Father of Bridget: PATRICK CLARKE (Farmer). Witnesses: Patrick Kennedy & Annie O'Malley. Officiant: Richard Duggan, P.P. Holy Cross, Dundrum. Record id cima-1271568. https://www.irishgenealogy.ie/view?record_id=cima-1271568

Research lead (unverified): Duleek Old Churchyard gravestone — Thomas Clarke (d.23 Jan 1877, age 45) & wife Bridget (d.25 Jan 1882, age 44) — possible PARENTS of Patrick Clarke Snr (i.e. paternal grandparents of Bridget Greene née Clarke). Source: https://meathhistoryhub.ie/duleek-old-churchyard/

people.name: Patrick Clarke
people.notes: Child of Patrick Clarke Snr and Catherine Clarke (née Clinton) of Mullaghfin, Duleek DED, Co. Meath. BROTHER (not father) of Bridget Clarke (later Bridget Greene, m. Thomas Christopher Greene). Almost certainly named after his father Patrick Clarke Snr (the labourer of Mullaghfin, deceased before 1901). Recorded in 1901 Census of Ireland, House 3 Mullaghfin, age 18, General Labourer, literacy: Read and write. Roman Catholic. Born Co. Meath. Further life unknown.
sources: 1901 Census of Ireland (PRIMARY SOURCE, retrieved 2026): Mullaghfin, Duleek DED, Co. Meath, House 3 — http://www.census.nationalarchives.ie/pages/1901/Meath/Duleek/Mullaghfin/1609301/ ; original Form A image: http://www.census.nationalarchives.ie/reels/nai001112178/

married.place: Roman Catholic Church of Holy Cross, Dundrum, Co. Dublin (Rathdown RD)
County Meath (presumed; Drogheda/Navan/Duleek area — civil record search in progress)
Church of St Mary and St Peter Vauxhall, St. Helier, Jersey

events.title: Earliest Record
events.description: Thos Green appears in Tithe Applotment Books, Ballyburly, King's County — approximately 8km north of Portarlington.

events.title: Thomas Greene Born
events.description: Born c. July 1870 in Portarlington (age 55 years 9 months in the April 1926 Census). Roman Catholic. His birthplace recorded as 'Queens Co./Portarlington' in the 1926 Census.

events.title: Mary Bridget Greene Born
events.description: Born in Portarlington, Laois. Daughter of Joseph Greene and Elizabeth Foran.

events.title: Thomas Enlists Underage
events.description: Thomas lied about his age and enlisted in the British Army at around age 14 — a practice that was remarkably common in the late Victorian army, especially among boys from poor rural backgrounds with few prospects. He likely joined the Prince of Wales's Leinster Regiment (Royal Canadians) at Birr Barracks, just 25 miles from Portarlington.

events.title: Thomas Marries Elizabeth
events.description: Thomas Greene marries Elizabeth (surname unknown) in Dublin. She is from County Dublin and approximately 7 years his senior.

events.title: Second Boer War
events.description: The Leinster Regiment served in South Africa. Thomas may have served before or during this conflict. Both the 1st and 2nd Battalions saw action at Hammonia, Harrismith, Vrede, and Bethlehem.

events.title: 1901 Census
events.description: Thomas (30) and Elizabeth (30) recorded at Ranelagh Avenue, Rathmines & Rathgar East, Dublin. Thomas is a Shop Porter. No children yet.

events.title: 1911 Census — Greene Family
events.description: Sunday 2 April 1911. Thomas (40) and Elizabeth (41) at Clonliffe Avenue, Mountjoy, Dublin (Form A; Form B household No. 1842). Thomas is now a Gentleman's Club Steward. Three children: Thomas Jr (10), Patrick (8), Elizabeth (4). Married 12 years. All children living. All Roman Catholic; all read & write except 4-year-old Elizabeth. Thomas's birthplace recorded as Kings Co. / Portarlington (Offaly side of the town). The form is signed by Thomas Greene himself — confirming he was literate. Enumerator: John Broderick.

events.title: 1911 Census — Bridget Clarke, Cook in Dublin
events.description: Sunday 2 April 1911. Bridget Clarke (19, Single, Roman Catholic, born Co. Meath, can read and write) is recorded on Form A in the Dublin household of Joseph Dollard, Justice of the Peace and Printer-Master (Form B No. 142, enumerator Robert J. Holmes). Her occupation: COOK (Domestic) — an unusually senior role for a 19-year-old. The household consists of Joseph (40, JP & Printer-Master), his wife Sarah (34, married 15 years), three daughters Mary (14, Scholar), Kathleen (13, Scholar) and baby Sarah (3 months); a Nursery Governess Ellen Clancy (24, also from Co. Meath); Bridget as Cook; and a Housemaid Margaret Brien (20, Co. Wexford). Joseph Dollard is almost certainly connected to the well-known Dollard's Printing House of Wellington Quay, Dublin. This 1911 Form A — discovered April 2026 — confirms the family oral history that Bridget worked as a cook in Dublin before her marriage to Thomas Greene, and supersedes an earlier (wrong) attribution to a different Bridget Clarke serving in Dr Christopher Waters' household.

events.title: 1926 Census — York Street, Dublin
events.description: The first census of the Irish Free State, enumerated 18 April 1926. Thomas Greene (Head, age 55y 9m, from Queens Co./Portarlington) is living at York Street, Dublin, with his second wife Bridget (age 34y 4m, from Duleek, Co. Meath). Thomas works as Shop Porter at Switzers & Co. Drapers on Grafton Street. Also in the household: Patrick Greene (son from first marriage, age 23, single, Battery Repairer/Motor Engineer for S.F. Robinson), John Greene (age 2y 9m), and baby Thomas Greene (age 0y 9m). The family occupies 2 rooms. All are Roman Catholic. Notable: Thomas Jr (~25) and Elizabeth (~19) from the first marriage are not in the household. Signed by Thomas Greene; Enumerator: Myles Cullen.

events.title: Elizabeth Moves to Channel Islands
events.description: Elizabeth Bessie Greene leaves Dublin and arrives on the island of Jersey (Channel Islands), where she works as a nurse. The exact year and route is not yet documented.

events.title: German Occupation of Jersey
events.description: German forces occupy the Channel Islands on 30 June 1940. Elizabeth Greene is on Jersey at the time and remains there throughout the occupation. Approximately 66,000 civilians stay on the islands; the British government had withdrawn shortly before, leaving the islands undefended.

events.title: Elizabeth Marries Alfred Harris — During the Occupation
events.description: On 13 September 1941, fourteen months into the German Occupation, Elizabeth Bessie Greene (34, Nurse, Spinster) marries Alfred Bower Harris (28, Printer, Bachelor, born Bretforton, Worcestershire) at the Church of St Mary and St Peter Vauxhall, in the Parish of St. Helier, Jersey. The ceremony is officiated by Richard T. Arnett with witnesses F.C. Colligny and C.M. Colligny. Both bride and groom give their address as St. Saviour's, Jersey. Her father Thomas Greene is recorded as 'Retired'. The marriage is registered as Page 97 of the Jersey 1941 register by Deputy Registrar W.A. de la Perrelle. Marrying under enemy occupation — with no telegrams home to Dublin, no ability for family to attend — is itself a remarkable act.

events.title: Deportation
events.description: On Hitler's direct orders (issued 15 September 1942), approximately 2,300 Channel Islands civilians are deported to internment camps in Germany. Those born outside the islands — including Elizabeth (born Dublin) and her new husband Alfred (born Worcestershire, England) — are primary targets. Both are sent to Biberach Internment Camp (Ilag V-B) in southern Germany. Their marriage of barely 12 months is followed straight into internment.

events.title: Biberach Internment
events.description: Elizabeth works as a nurse at the Men's Hospital of Biberach Internment Camp under Dr. A.W.H. Donaldson, M.B., D.P.H. Despite harsh conditions and rationed food, she is described as 'keen, energetic and efficient, and always cheerful.'

events.title: Liberation
events.description: Biberach is liberated by the French First Army on 23 April 1945. On 21 May, Dr. Donaldson writes a glowing letter of recommendation for Nurse Harris, praising her nursing abilities, kindness, and even her cooking — 'her cakes and other dainties have been a god-send where the rationed food has been served up with so little of variety.'

events.title: Thomas Greene Dies
events.description: Thomas Christopher Greene dies on 6 December 1951, age 81, at 38 Goldenbridge Avenue, Inchicore, Dublin. Cause of death: carcinoma of the stomach, cachexia, cardiac failure. Still listed as a Warehouse Porter — a working man to the very end. His death was registered the following day by his son John Greene, who was living at the same address. Registrar: M. Ryan.

events.title: Bridget Greene Dies
events.description: Bridget Greene (née Clarke) dies on 9 August 1970, age 78, at Adelaide Hospital, Dublin, admitted from 38 Goldenbridge Avenue, Inchicore — still living in the family home nearly 19 years after Thomas's death. Cause of death: acute massive gangrene of small intestine and cardiac failure. Widowed. Death registered 11 August 1970 by her son Brendan Greene of 62 Limekiln Drive, Terenure.

events.title: Bridget Clarke Born — Registered at Duleek
events.description: Bridget Clarke is born on 2 December 1891 at Mullaghfin, in the District of Duleek (Union of Drogheda, County Meath). Her father is Pat Clark, Labourer, of Mullaghfin. Her mother is Catherine Clarke, formerly CLINTON. The birth is registered on 26 December 1891 by registrar James Kelly; the informant is Julia Boylan, who was present at the birth (she signed with her mark "+" — illiterate). The registration is later certified as a true copy in October 1892 and again January 1893 by the Superintendent Registrar. CONFIRMED (April 2026) from IrishGenealogy.ie record civbi-9688899. This is the document that definitively establishes Bridget's mother's maiden name as CLINTON (correcting an earlier false-positive McKenna lead from a different Co. Meath couple).

events.title: Catherine Clarke Widowed
events.description: Catherine Clarke's husband PATRICK CLARKE — labourer of Mullaghfin, recorded as alive on the 26 December 1891 registration of Bridget's birth — dies sometime between then and the 1901 Census, leaving her a widow with six children in Mullaghfin, Duleek. She works as a general labourer — one of the hardest lives imaginable for a woman in 1890s rural Ireland. She cannot read or write. (His exact death year and cause are not yet documented; civil death registers for Drogheda/Duleek RD remain to be searched.)

events.title: Thomas Marries Bridget Clarke — Holy Cross, Dundrum
events.description: On 20 June 1922 — six months into the existence of the new Irish Free State — Thomas Greene (Widower, Warehouseman, age "Full") married Bridget Clarke (Spinster, Housekeeper, age "Full") at the Roman Catholic Church of Holy Cross, Dundrum, in the Registrar's District of Dundrum (Union of Rathdown, Co. Dublin). Bridget was working as housekeeper at Drummartin House in Dundrum at the time; Thomas had given an address in Dublin city. Witnesses: Patrick Kennedy and Annie O'Malley. Officiant: Richard Duggan, P.P. The marriage register (entry 89) names Thomas's father as William Greene (Traveller) and Bridget's father as Patrick Clarke (Farmer). They went on to have four children: John, Thomas, Brendan, and Ita. CONFIRMED (April 2026) from IrishGenealogy.ie record cima-1271568.

events.title: Move to Goldenbridge Avenue
events.description: The Greene family moves from York Street tenements to 38 Goldenbridge Avenue, Inchicore — part of Dublin Corporation's ambitious housing programme replacing inner-city slums with purpose-built suburban homes. This house will remain the family home for over 90 years.

events.title: Thomas Patrick Greene Dies in New Zealand
events.description: Thomas Patrick Greene (II), born 21 July 1925 in Dublin, dies in New Zealand at age 75. He had emigrated to New Zealand, married Margaret (a Samoan woman), and raised a family whose descendants now live across New Zealand and Australia. NZ BDM registration: 2000/1764.

events.title: Brendan Greene Dies
events.description: Brendan Greene dies on 3 March 2023 at Limekiln Road, Terenure, Dublin. Married Roisin (predeceased). Three children: Terry, Geraldine, and Dermot (predeceased). Brendan's move from Inchicore to the middle-class suburb of Terenure represented the upward social mobility of his generation.

events.title: Ita Donnelly Dies — End of an Era
events.description: Ita Donnelly (née Greene, Eithne) dies peacefully on 6 December 2025 at Kiltipper Woods Care Centre, aged 91. She was the last of Thomas and Bridget's children. Remarkably, she had lived at 38 Goldenbridge Avenue, Inchicore — the family home — her entire life. Survived by children Ann, John, Sheila, and Ger, nine grandchildren including Claire (née Donnelly, wife of researcher Mark Garrigan), and great-grandchildren. Funeral at St. Michael's Church, Inchicore; buried Deansgrange Cemetery. Donations to the Alzheimer Society of Ireland.

events.title: 1901 Census — Clarke household, Mullaghfin, Duleek, Co. Meath
events.description: Catherine Clarke (40, widow, General Labourer, cannot read, Roman Catholic, Head of Family) at House 3 Mullaghfin, Duleek DED, Co. Meath, with six children: Rose (23, Gen. Labourer), Patrick (18, Gen. Labourer, literate), Mary (12, Scholar), Jane (10, Scholar), Bridget (9, Scholar — later Bridget Greene, Mark Garrigan's great-grandmother), Agnes (5, Scholar). All Roman Catholic, born Co. Meath. Source: http://www.census.nationalarchives.ie/pages/1901/Meath/Duleek/Mullaghfin/1609301/ ; Form A image: http://www.census.nationalarchives.ie/reels/nai001112178/
